pi_items.source: SQLite query on Chinook database: PRAGMA table_info on Customer table and Invoice table structure analysis
SQLite query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerId) FROM Customer
SQLite query: SELECT ROUND(SUM(Total), 2) as GrandTotal FROM Invoice; Result: 2328.6 (equivalent to 2328.60)
SQLite query on Chinook database showing customer ID 6 (Helena Holý from Czech Republic) has the highest total spending
SQLite query: Customer ID 6 (Helena Holý) total spending = 49.62 from SUM of Invoice.Total where CustomerId=6
Cannot verify without running the task in the GUI environment; path specification is reasonable for the task setup
Tolerance is a verification parameter for floating-point comparison, not a property of the data itself

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database contains exactly 59 customers. The total spending across all invoices is $2328.60. Customer ID 6 (Helena Holý from Czech Republic) is the top spender with $49.62 in total purchases. The required query columns (CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email, Country, and TotalSpending) correspond directly to Customer table and aggregated Invoice data.
